mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5%, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses above 6.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term interest rates combined with accelerating retail sales indicate strong consumer demand outpacing monetary tightening, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production signals strengthening supply-side capacity and corporate output growth, which translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 during recessionary periods or when quarterly GDP growth turns negative.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor market expansion and rising household income, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of months following positive payroll revisions across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 255.0, elevated inflation erodes real corporate margins and forces aggressive monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day forward returns in more than 65% of instances when the CPI threshold is breached.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside falling unemployment indicate synchronized strength in residential investment and labor absorption, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signals broad-based economic expansion and rising corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter during expansionary macro regimes.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in more than 50% of quarters when annualized GDP growth exceeds 2.0% during mid-cycle phases.
